questions.question: Why must a full stack developer understand both client and server responsibilities?
options: A) Because frontend automatically replaces the backend
B) Because user experience, data flow, performance, and failures often cross the client-server boundary
C) Because databases are optional in most apps
D) Because APIs are only a design concern
questions.correctAnswer: B) Because user experience, data flow, performance, and failures often cross the client-server boundary

questions.question: What does HTTP primarily provide in a web application?
options: A) A styling engine for CSS
B) A request-response protocol used by clients and servers to exchange web resources
C) A database indexing method
D) A JavaScript package manager
questions.correctAnswer: B) A request-response protocol used by clients and servers to exchange web resources

questions.question: Why is semantic HTML important in full stack work?
options: A) It only matters for backend APIs
B) It improves structure, accessibility, and maintainability for user-facing interfaces
C) It replaces application state
D) It removes the need for CSS
questions.correctAnswer: B) It improves structure, accessibility, and maintainability for user-facing interfaces

questions.question: What is the practical role of JavaScript in the browser?
options: A) Managing interactivity, state changes, async requests, and client-side logic
B) Compiling SQL queries directly on the server
C) Replacing HTML semantics
D) Creating DNS records
questions.correctAnswer: A) Managing interactivity, state changes, async requests, and client-side logic

questions.question: Why are APIs central to full stack applications?
options: A) They let the browser edit source code files on disk
B) They define how clients and servers exchange data and actions in a structured way
C) They only matter for mobile games
D) They remove the need for databases
questions.correctAnswer: B) They define how clients and servers exchange data and actions in a structured way

questions.question: What is validation meant to protect against in backend routes?
options: A) Only slow internet connections
B) Invalid or malicious input reaching business logic and storage layers
C) CSS layout issues
D) Git merge conflicts
questions.correctAnswer: B) Invalid or malicious input reaching business logic and storage layers

questions.question: What is the main difference between authentication and authorization?
options: A) Authentication identifies who a user is, authorization decides what they can do
B) Authorization happens before login and authentication after deployment
C) They are identical
D) Authentication only matters in desktop apps
questions.correctAnswer: A) Authentication identifies who a user is, authorization decides what they can do

questions.question: Why is database indexing important?
options: A) It changes the UI theme
B) It can improve query performance for targeted lookups and filters
C) It removes the need for schema design
D) It only applies to frontend state
questions.correctAnswer: B) It can improve query performance for targeted lookups and filters

questions.question: What problem does state management solve on the frontend?
options: A) It manages changing UI data and keeps views consistent as user actions and async results arrive
B) It replaces server-side persistence completely
C) It disables routing
D) It is only needed for CSS variables
questions.correctAnswer: A) It manages changing UI data and keeps views consistent as user actions and async results arrive

questions.question: Why does CORS matter in web development?
options: A) It controls how browsers handle cross-origin requests to protect users
B) It is a package manager feature
C) It stores session data in SQL
D) It compiles TypeScript
questions.correctAnswer: A) It controls how browsers handle cross-origin requests to protect users

questions.question: What is the main value of environment variables in an application?
options: A) Keeping configuration separate from source code and deployment environments
B) Styling production pages
C) Replacing API routes
D) Encrypting databases automatically
questions.correctAnswer: A) Keeping configuration separate from source code and deployment environments

questions.question: Why should passwords never be stored as plain text?
options: A) Because databases cannot hold strings
B) Because leaked storage would immediately expose user credentials without hashing protections
C) Because only frontend apps use passwords
D) Because tokens make passwords illegal
questions.correctAnswer: B) Because leaked storage would immediately expose user credentials without hashing protections

questions.question: What is a useful reason to write automated tests in a full stack app?
options: A) They replace code review completely
B) They help verify critical logic and reduce regressions as the application changes
C) They make deployment unnecessary
D) They only matter for CSS
questions.correctAnswer: B) They help verify critical logic and reduce regressions as the application changes

questions.question: What does CI/CD improve in a delivery workflow?
options: A) It automates verification and deployment steps so releases are more repeatable
B) It removes the need for source control
C) It replaces observability
D) It only benefits static HTML pages
questions.correctAnswer: A) It automates verification and deployment steps so releases are more repeatable

questions.question: Why is logging valuable on the backend?
options: A) It only helps after deleting a database
B) It supports debugging, monitoring, and investigation when systems misbehave
C) It replaces error handling
D) It is only for frontend animations
questions.correctAnswer: B) It supports debugging, monitoring, and investigation when systems misbehave

questions.question: What is the benefit of pagination in API design?
options: A) It reduces the need for authentication
B) It controls response size and improves usability and performance on large datasets
C) It guarantees zero latency
D) It styles tables automatically
questions.correctAnswer: B) It controls response size and improves usability and performance on large datasets

questions.question: Why does a production deployment need monitoring in addition to local testing?
options: A) Because real traffic, infrastructure issues, and runtime failures appear only after deployment
B) Because local testing is useless
C) Because monitoring builds the UI
D) Because monitoring replaces logs
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because real traffic, infrastructure issues, and runtime failures appear only after deployment

questions.question: What is the main purpose of Docker in a full stack workflow?
options: A) It lets developers package applications and dependencies into consistent runnable environments
B) It is a replacement for databases
C) It compiles JavaScript faster in the browser
D) It prevents every security issue
questions.correctAnswer: A) It lets developers package applications and dependencies into consistent runnable environments

questions.question: Why is API contract consistency important between frontend and backend teams?
options: A) It reduces integration failures by keeping request and response expectations aligned
B) It removes the need for version control
C) It only matters in monoliths
D) It is only a database issue
questions.correctAnswer: A) It reduces integration failures by keeping request and response expectations aligned

questions.question: What is the strongest mindset for someone learning full stack development?
options: A) Memorize frameworks only
B) Build strong fundamentals, understand system boundaries, and ship real end-to-end projects
C) Focus on backend only and ignore UI
D) Avoid debugging distributed issues
questions.correctAnswer: B) Build strong fundamentals, understand system boundaries, and ship real end-to-end projects
